name: water_utility_lead_service_line_priority
premise: A city receives limited federal funds to replace lead service lines but can only cover one-third of known homes this year. Utilities director Hannah Alvarez has two competing maps: one based on blood lead screenings and one based on pipe age. The health map points to neighborhoods with higher poverty and more renters, but landlords are slower to grant access and replacements may trigger rent increases. The pipe-age map is simpler and would hit older, wealthier areas first. A state auditor warns that any deviation from 'objective criteria' could be challenged politically. Community leaders demand a plan that prevents displacement and does not repeat past infrastructure neglect. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Civil Infrastructure.

agents.name: Hannah Alvarez
agents.identity: I am the utilities director allocating limited replacement funds when criteria choices can either reduce harm or reinforce inequality.
agents.goal: I want the plan to prevent lead exposure without triggering displacement or being derailed by political attacks on fairness.

agents.name: Dr. Malik Johnson
agents.identity: I am the public health commissioner interpreting screening data and advocating for communities with the highest exposure burden.
agents.goal: I want replacements prioritized by actual health risk even if implementation is harder, because convenience should not trump harm prevention.

choices: I will prioritize replacements using health-risk data and pair it with tenant protections and landlord enforcement, accepting slower throughput to reduce real exposure.
I will prioritize replacements by pipe-age criteria to maximize completion speed and audit defensibility, accepting that health disparities may persist longer.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Funding is limited and every criterion will be contested. Hannah Alvarez must choose between health-risk prioritization with protections or pipe-age prioritization for speed and audit defensibility. What does Hannah Alvarez do?
